description: The chart claims to show "Number of Orders per Month (2025)" but only displays May through November, cherry-picking a 7-month window that starts from a moderate level (~110 in May), dips through the summer (~91 in August), and then surges dramatically to ~195 in September — creating a narrative of strong late-year growth. The omitted months (January, February, March, April, and December) hide the fact that the year started very low (~45-50 orders) and ended high (~185 in December), which would show the full-year trend more accurately and contextualize the September spike differently.
